title: URL References, Resolution, Identity Keys, Unicode, and Canonicalization · Runtime Lens: Reference Resolution
summary: Relative references, fragments, default ports, dot segments, percent encodings, Unicode hosts, and tracking parameters produce duplicates or escape scope. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reference resolution increment in URL resolution and identity pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.
Parse scheme, authority, userinfo, host, port, path, query, and fragment without treating a URL as a trustworthy string.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-url-identity-canonicalization-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-url-identity-canonicalization-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_url_identity_canonicalization_2(references: int = 24, resolved: int = 24, fetch_keys: int = 18, unsafe_merges: int = 0, idna_failures_visible: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if references < 1 or resolved < 0:
        return False, resolved, "url-count"
    if resolved != references:
        return False, resolved, "resolution-gap"
    if fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved:
        return False, fetch_keys, "identity-key"
    if unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1:
        return False, unsafe_merges, "url-security"
    assert resolved == references and unsafe_merges == 0
    return True, fetch_keys, "conservative-url-identity"

options.text: For repair the reference resolution defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Reference Resolution, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the reference resolution defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Reference Resolution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 6322 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution, keep Reference Resolution connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Reference Resolution precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 6322 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Reference Resolution model · Theory Reference Resolution, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: String concatenation correctly resolves every relative link.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-reference-resolution-1drjeu(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_reference_resolution_1drjeu\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?references < 1 or resolved < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resolved != references)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
